commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=233b7dbfbc3b2dabde38953b0b9226114d9168f1)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes provider runtime error classification so Cloudflare challenge 403 HTML responses return `upstream_html` while generic 403 HTML remains `auth_html`.

PR surface: Source +13, Tests +11. Total +24 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the classifier boundary by source inspection: current main maps a status 403 HTML body to auth_html. No live Codex OAuth request was run, so the full upstream transport failure remains reporter-provided evidence.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer action is needed because the branch has a concrete code finding and still lacks real behavior proof that automation cannot supply for the contributor's setup.

target.title: fix: #94432 classify Cloudflare challenge 403 as upstream_html instead of auth_html
target.body:
## Summary

- Detect Cloudflare browser-challenge HTML 403 responses from chatgpt.com/backend-api
- Reclassify them as `upstream_html` instead of `auth_html`, surfacing a more accurate error message

## Problem

When the Codex provider makes server-side requests to `https://chatgpt.com/backend-api`, Cloudflare returns an HTML browser-challenge page with status 403. The error classification in `classifyProviderRuntimeFailureKind` treated ALL 403 HTML responses as `auth_html`, producing the misleading message:

> "Authentication failed at the provider. Re-authenticate and verify your provider credentials and account access."

This is incorrect — the Cloudflare challenge is a CDN/gateway block, not an authentication failure.

## Fix

- Added `CLOUDFLARE_CHALLENGE_RE` regex and `isCloudflareChallengeResponse()` helper to detect Cloudflare challenge pages (matching "Enable JavaScript and cookies to continue", "cf-browser-verification", "__cf_challenge")
- In the `classifyProviderRuntimeFailureKind` function, Cloudflare challenge 403 is now classified as `upstream_html`, which surfaces the accurate message:

> "The provider returned an HTML error page instead of an API response. This usually means a CDN or gateway (e.g. Cloudflare) blocked the request."

- Generic 403 HTML responses (non-Cloudflare) remain classified as `auth_html` — no regression.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Cloudflare browser-challenge 403 responses from chatgpt.com/backend-api are now classified as `upstream_html` (CDN/gateway block) instead of `auth_html` (authentication failure), surfacing accurate error messaging.

**Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64 (kernel 4.19.112), Node.js v26.2.0, openclaw main @ HEAD (built from `fix/issue-94432-cloudflare-challenge-error` branch)

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Run the classification unit test suite with `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/provider-error-patterns.test.ts` — this executes the existing 80-test suite including the new Cloudflare challenge classification test and the generic 403 regression test, all against the compiled production source via vitest import resolution.

**After-fix evidence**: All 80 tests pass after the fix:
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/provider-error-patterns.test.ts

 ✓ src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/provider-error-patterns.test.ts (80 tests) 487ms

   ✓ classifies Cloudflare challenge 403 as upstream_html
   ✓ classifies generic 403 HTML runtime failures as auth_html
   ✓ classifies Cloudflare HTML 502 as timeout
   ✓ classifies 401 HTML as auth
   ... (76 additional existing tests pass, no regressions)

Test Files  1 passed (1 file)
    Tests  80 passed (80)
```

**Observed result after the fix**: All 80 tests pass. The Cloudflare challenge 403 is now correctly classified as `upstream_html`. The generic (non-Cloudflare) 403 HTML remains classified as `auth_html` — confirming no regression. This means users hitting Cloudflare browser challenges will see the accurate message: "The provider returned an HTML error page instead of an API response... CDN or gateway blocked the request."

**What was not tested**: End-to-end test with a real `chatgpt.com/backend-api` request that triggers an actual Cloudflare challenge (requires live network access to a Cloudflare-protected endpoint and a scenario where the challenge is triggered). The classification logic for the challenge regex is verified by unit tests with realistic Cloudflare challenge HTML samples.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm vitest run src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/provider-error-patterns.test.ts` — 80 tests passed
- New test: "classifies Cloudflare challenge 403 as upstream_html" — verifies the new behavior
- Existing test: "classifies generic 403 HTML runtime failures as auth_html" — verifies no regression
- Existing tests: "classifies Cloudflare HTML 502 as timeout", "classifies 401 HTML as auth" — unchanged behavior

## Related Issue

Closes #94432
